title: Far Cry 3

notes:
Like others, you need to use a crack to avoid Uplay.  Just google search "far cry 3 crack" and there's a roughly 20MB zip from megagames that worked for me.  The game has an error when starting up, but otherwise it seems to play very smoothly.
Part of me wonders if instead you could try installing Uplay within the wineprefix that the game installed in, but I don't feel so confident in that since Uplay searches for the Windows version of Steam.  It's a lot of work to install Uplay in Wine, so I didn't bother testing this.
title: Far Cry 3

title: Far Cry 3

notes: The uPlay launcher did not launch with an older Proton prefix. Deleting it fixed the issue. Story mode and general gameplay is nearly flawless - there are some shadows rendered incorrectly. In addition, some features like Co-op rely on uPlay game overlay, which needs to be disabled for the game to launch.
title: Far Cry 3

notes: Requires a dxvk.conf with "dxgi.nvapiHack = False" to work around an issue with wierd shadow rendering through walls. Get a steady FPS, but it has some microstuttering. I reduced said stuttering by disabling motion blur, as suggested on the PCGW (https://pcgamingwiki.com/wiki/Far_Cry_3#Disable_motion_blur), but I removed 'FXAALevel="0"' from the settings suggested there since it appeared to have a negative effect. I also disabled vsync and gpu frame buffering in the game settings, and used the "-RenderProfile_MaxFPS 60" command-line parameter to limit the fps to 60.
title: Far Cry 3

notes: As of 3.16-8, it no longer needs the dxvk.conf change. Runs fairly well out of the box. Still needs tweaks for performance, such as disabling motion blur. I did complete the game from start to finish using proton, though, so it's a pretty good experience.

notes: I disabled UPlay overlay as recommended below. The game works perfectly  on default graphic settings without any framedrops. The game saves also work properly.
title: Far Cry 3

notes: Ran great after I disabled the UPlay overlay. You do this by changing the settings in the UPlay app that launches before the game. I did not have to install a crack. Steam thinks the game is still running after it exits, but that is probably because the UPlay app is still running.
title: Far Cry 3

notes: WHY ARE ALL THE OTHER DESCRIPTION SO UNHELPFUL? Most of them tell to disable the uplay overlay, but if you have already installed the game and selected "remember me" on the login it isn't even possible to do without wine trickery, because the game gets to a black screen! So a novice user would NOT know what to do. If you get to this state, simply uninstall the game and install it again via Steam, so that it asks your data again. So, after the game is installed it will launch UPLAY, log in with your account but DON'T check "Remember me"; input or paste the key to Far Cry 3 that steam has shown you before launching, and then, BEFORE exiting uplay, go to SETTINGS and set to always start in offline mode, and also disable uplay overlay and everything else you can see that would be related to online play, like chat and news. Once you ensure these settings are as I told, exit the UPLAY launcher and play the game. It will work correctly except for multiplayer. After you play and exit, the game will still show as running; go to a terminal and type (without the quotes): "pkill -fi 'pnkb|crrender|fc3|far cry|wine|\.exe' ; sleep 1 ; pkill -9 -f winedevice" -- that will kill all the game processes, and steam will detect that it stopped. I hope my instructions get noticed before newbies give up...
title: Far Cry 3

notes: After some tweaking, it now runs perfectly. First, set the launch options to "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%". Second, start the game. Third, go to the UPlay options and disable the in-game overlay. Fourth, exit UPlay and relaunch the game. UPlay will complain about the overlay being disabled. Run the game anyway. I just played for three hours straight and no crashes.
title: Far Cry 3

notes:
After disabling the Uplay Overlay the game mostly runs fine.

The exit problem: Steam thinks that the game still runs, because Uplay still runs. On my Arch Linux with KDE Uplay appears in the system tray. Right-click and choose quit to let steam know that Far Cry 3 doesn't run anymore.

I didn't spend a lot of time in the game, but I noticed that the crosshairs for weapons not using irons sights are missing (e.g. bow & sniper). Once I died and the game crashed. However, I was a bit impressed by the (so far) very good performance.

title: Far Cry 3

notes:
Runs great with tweaks at maximum settings at 1080p
Tweaks:
1) ¡¡¡VERY IMPORTANT!!! Disable everything UPLAY and make it run in offline mode at first launch (if you fail to do this you will have to remove and reinstall)
2) To stop the game from crashing at loading times put in launch options: PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%
3) When you quit the game you will see a small wine window that is running the windows system tray and UPLAY will be there, right click and quit UPLAY to effectively close the game.
4) No need to disable ESYNC
Notes:
1) There is a graphical glitch specially at night where you will see a dark semi-transparent square covering an area of your screen - this is a known glitch proton devs are aware and it will be solved in future versions of proton.

Have fun!
